segments.text: To the choirmaster: according to The Dove on Far-off Terebinths. A Michtam of David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath.

footnotes: Michtam: or, A golden Psalm of David

segments.text: Be gracious to me, O God, for man tramples on me; all day long an attacker oppresses me;
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: my enemies trample on me all day long, for many attack me proudly.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When I am afraid, I put my trust in you.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust; I shall not be afraid. What can flesh do to me?
All day long they injure my cause; all their thoughts are against me for evil.
They stir up strife, they lurk; they watch my steps, as they have waited for my life.
For their crime will they escape? In anger cast down the peoples, O God.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You have kept count of my tossings; put my tears in your bottle. Are they not in your book?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then my enemies will turn back in the day when I call. This I know, that God is for me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: In God, whose word I praise, in the Lord, whose word I praise,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: in God I trust; I shall not be afraid. What can man do to me?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I must perform my vows to you, O God; I will render thank offerings to you.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For you have delivered my soul from death, yes, my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of life.
segments.speaker: David
